name: Fettuccine Al Carciofi
recipeIngredient: 6 ounces Prosciutto, thinly sliced into strips
1 pound Fresh fettuccine (cook al dente, drain)
recipeInstructions:
1. Bend back the outer green petals of the artichokes until they snap off easily near the base.
2. Continue snapping off and discarding petals until the central core of pale green petals is reached.
3. Trim the outer dark green layer from the base using a stainless steel knife to minimize discoloration, then discard it.
4. Cut off the top one-third of the petals and discard them.
5. Cut the stem level with the base.
6. Halve each artichoke and slice each half into fourths, removing the hairy choke with a small knife. Place in a mixture of lemon juice and water while working on the remaining artichokes to retard discoloration.
7. Melt 2 tablespoons butter in a large skillet; sauté the artichokes for 5 to 10 minutes or until tender. Add 2 tablespoons water, cover, and simmer.
8. Melt the remaining butter in a large, heavy saucepan; sauté the prosciutto, shallots, pepper, and garlic for 5 minutes or until the pepper is tender.
9. Add wine and boil for about 3 minutes or until reduced by half.
10. Mix in cream and pesto. Cook for about 5 minutes or until the sauce thickens. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
11. Keep the sauce warm.
12. Place the pasta in a large serving bowl and top with cooked artichokes.
13. Pour the sauce over the top.
14. Add Parmesan cheese and toasted pine nuts.
15. Toss well and serve immediately.
